description: Accessibility invariants for JSX/HTML — the mechanically-checkable subset of 'good UI'. Missing alt/title, hrefless anchors, positive tabindex, and click handlers with no keyboard path are never intentional, so they block (HARD). The semantic half of UX (states, copy, contrast, focus order) lives in rules/ux-doctrine.md, not here. Patterns match per ADDED line: a tag split across multiple lines can slip through — keep the attribute on the tag's opening line, or add a repo-local override.

rules.message: with no alt attribute — every image needs alt text (alt="" for purely decorative images). Spread-prop images () are not flagged.
<a> with no href — a link without href is not keyboard-focusable or announced as a link. Use a real href, or use a <button> if it triggers an action. (<article>/<aside>/<audio> are not matched.)
<iframe> with no title — screen readers announce the title to describe the embedded content. Add title="...".
positive tabIndex — overrides natural DOM focus order and breaks keyboard navigation. Use 0 (focusable, natural order) or -1 (programmatically focusable only).
onClick on a non-interactive element with no role and no keyboard handler — not reachable by keyboard. Use <button>, or add role + tabIndex={0} + an onKeyDown/onKeyUp handler.
autoFocus moves focus on load — disorienting for screen-reader and keyboard users, and a WCAG focus-order concern. Justify per-use or focus deliberately in response to a user action.
